entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A cereal crop whose hybrid cultivars revolutionalized agriculture by increasing production and productivity.

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A crop for which hybrid parents research has been conducted at ICRISAT.

entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A crop for which hybrid parents research has been conducted at ICRISAT.

entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A crop where the discovery of the CMS system is enabling the development of hybrid cultivars.

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC-NUCLEAR MALE STERILITY
entities.description: A biological system used in hybrid breeding to facilitate the production of hybrid cultivars.

entities.name: HYBRID CULTIVARS
entities.description: Plant varieties resulting from the crossing of different parents to improve productivity and production.

entities.name: HYBRID BREEDING
entities.description: The agricultural process of creating hybrid cultivars, often utilizing CMS systems.

entities.name: HYBRID PARENTS RESEARCH
entities.description: Research focused on the development and evolution of parents used in hybrid breeding.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The research institution conducting hybrid parents research for various crops.

entities.name: DISEASE
entities.description: Pathological threats that can be mitigated by diversifying CMS systems in hybrid breeding.

entities.name: INSECT PEST
entities.description: Harmful organisms that can be mitigated by diversifying CMS systems in hybrid breeding.

entities.name: PUBLIC-PRIVATE PARTNERSHIP
entities.description: A collaborative model used to ensure the availability of hybrid seeds to farmers.

entities.name: RESOURCE-POOR FARMERS
entities.description: The target group for the distribution of affordable hybrid cultivar seeds.
